edus.text: And where are they now? Serious question. I wonder if they changed names and locations or shut down.

edus.text: Right, seems like a far fetched name. Home games have been played at Lambeau Field since 1957.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes, in Green Bay, which is not an especially big city. I hope the Packers go on forever. They are a really unique team with great traditions.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Did you know the highrd game scored was 220-0
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes! And it was a college game! Georgia Tech versus Cumberland. I bet there was more than 11 minutes of game play. What do you think?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I wonder how long it was. NFL only has 11 mins of game play. Foot ball is different in other countries. We know them as soccer or rugby.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes. It's confusing in countries where soccer and rugby are both called football.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Do you like fantasy football? Did you know people fantasy draff the spelling bee?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes, I just learned about that recently! There is also a Fantasy Congress!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Talking about fantasy. A single fold in the brain makes the difference between perceiving reality and fantasy
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes! It's true! Well, use that fold well and have a great day wherever you are!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: This whole topic is really tough. Moonves just got fired for cause and lost millions!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Sexual misconduct is a very serious charge and this must really affect the entertainment industry considering his role.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah. I just am so sad for all the victims. It puts them in such a bad spot.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Having lots of power it must be hard for the victims to stand up to someone in such a position.  Harvey Weinstein stories of women set the bar high
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah... on . lighter note, When south park aired its family guy episode finale the producers received flowers from the simpsons crew
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's a sweet gesture.  There are actually 750 episodes of Pokemon.  Have you seen any?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I have in passing... i have a 7 year old. How about you? Pokemon fan?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: kids used to like it but outgrown I guess.  There is only one episode of the Simpsons without Bart
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I did not know that... 70's show was remade verbatim in the uk. 'days like these' was cancelled after 10 episodes.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: No woman was ever nominated for best cinematagrapher academy award.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Really, that has to be strange not have a women nominated. I feel like they should have been nominated, at least.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes, you would think.  Cinematographer Roger Deakins though has been nominated 13 times without a win for the best cinematographer oscar
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: When caesar visited britain, around 50bc, he noted that its people bred chickens not for food, but for entertainment
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I wonder how they entertained? Bill nye seemed to entertain and he got a break in entertainment by winning a steve martin lookalike contest.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah... Ronald reagan was once rejected for a movie role because an entertainment executive didn't think he had "that presidential look."
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is funny!  The nintendo entertainment system was discontinued in 2003 in Japan.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Really? What about the switch. Seems weird.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Maybe that means the original?  It talks about the entertainment system only displaying 54 colors.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I did not know that. Only 54?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Entertainment like storytelling, music, drama, dance and other performances exist in all cultures
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Nice chatting with you.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hi, do you use facebook?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I have an account but I rarely access it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I feel like I am on it all the time.  I manage several business pages though, so that takes up time
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: My wife uses it somewhat, I guess I'm just an unsocial person.  Plus I have reservations about it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I can see that.  Do you know why it is blue?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: You mean the logo?  I assume it has to do with the preference of the creators.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: It does, Zuckerberg has red-green colorblindness.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I know it's contrary , but I kind of like that promotion Burger King did, unfriend 10 people and get a free Whopper.  Do they have issues with Facebook?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I dont think they do, but it is definitely a thing to get people in the door.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The way Facebook is become ubiquitous I can imagine that we'll be voting through them one day!  I guess we kind of do with the whole Like button ting.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, except for some people in Germany.  It is illegal there.  I wonder how that works.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I've heard the Europeans and it's government are a lot stricter with privacy rules there too.  You can request to see what information is being collected on you and also restrict it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That is neat, I wish some of those rules applied here.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: There has been the same concerns about interference in elections, such as with the Irish referendum.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I heard that.  Facebook restricted ads during that time
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The referendum was very sensitive to Ireland, a clash between the young and the old, and the religious and not so.  I think they wanted to keep outside forces out of it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: They really wanted for things to be transparent.  Having participated in creating ads, they have some pretty strict policies, I am surprised things related to this even got through their filters honestly.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It must be difficult to know the sources of anything now.  In the age of the internet media can be made anywhere and by anyone.  How do you know it's original source?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: It is hard to know.  I think that is why they ask users to flag questionable things, so that there can be further review.  It still doesnt prevent the slanted truths from coming out though
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: There appears to be a  lot more over sight of Facebook here now.  With stories keep on cropping up about sharing of data more and more people are becoming concerned about it.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Definitely true.  Ive got to go, its been nice chatting.  Have a great day!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Do you follow politics at all?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Hi there!  A little bit.  I find odd facts interesting such as Ralph Lawrence Carr.  He was the only governor to oppose the internment of Japanese Americans during WW2.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Doesn't that make you wonder about the morality of the American people at the time? I suppose that fear mongering and mistrust held sway.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes.  When I was a kid my grandmother worked with a lady who was interred.  I got to know her as well and she was the sweetest lady.  I never realized though exactly what they went through until I got older.  Sad indeed about the people back then.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes, I read quite a bit about that time. There have been many books written, not so much about the camps themselves but the results of them. I find it fascinating. For awhile I was worried that the same might be done with Muslims because of the climate after 9/11. Oh, well. Did you know that ND's governor was tried to secede from the union. Wish I knew what that was about.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I didn't know about the ND.  The Beatles song "Come Together" was written for Timothy Leary's 1969 California governor campaign.  I never knew that.  Interesting that they wrote a song that had to do with American politics.  Lennon must have been living here at the time.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Could have been. I didn't know that Leary did anything. I live in Wa and our Gov. just proclaimed for the Presidency. Nice guy but I'm not sure how he would work out.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Did you know that 86 percent of the land in Nevada is owned by the US govt?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes, I believe that Alaska has the same circumstance but a lower percentage. I'm all for holding land in trust to protect it from commercialization. I think that ensures that our descendants will have open spaces and unspoiled wilderness. I Have to laugh at some of the people who get elected, like the 'Governator'.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: lol  You must be talking about Arnold Schwarzenegger?  When he was governor of California he never lived in Sacramento but commuted 3 hours every day by private jet.  Crazy.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: When you're already rich what's a million or two spent on commuting? Our entire system of governance sometimes  leaves me shaking my head at the elitism that has been embraced. I wish we could get another party going, or do away with them altogether.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I'm kind of with you there.  There hasn't been a US president who wasn't a republican or a democrat since 1853.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yes, and as they get more entrenched the divide grows deeper. There have been good men elected on both sides but no one will admit that someone else has a few good ideas.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Yes.  I never knew that every US president with a beard has been a republican.  Did you know that?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I did not, probably against their belief system to shave. I also didn't know that the Red/Blue labeling system only began to be used in the year 2000. I would have sworn that it had always been there.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I'm with you on that.  I seem to remember that being around long before then.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Me too. I guess we become used to things like that rather rapidly. Now it has evolved into left/right Liberal/conservation, Urban/rural. What a mess.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It is.  I just found an interesting fact.  The last time a republican was elected president withou a nixon or bush on the ticket was 1928.  I don't think that includes the latest president though.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I would rather forget all about him in the hopes that he'll just go away but you're right. We seem to have created several dynasties. What amazes me is that voters will elect one party for the Presidency and another for Congressional seats.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: You're right.  I've always wondered about that.  Here's a funny tidbit....in 1957 President Harry Truman visited Disneyland and refused to be photographed on the Dumbo ride so he wouldn't be associated with Republicans.  lol
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: So it began way back then? I wish that they would act more for the American people and less for their constituency. I lean heavily Democratic but even I have to admit that each President has had his moment in the sun.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I agree.  What saddens me is that it seems like every politician never follows through on what they say they want to do when they are campaigning.  I have to get going. I enjoyed talking politics with you!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Hi, how are you today?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Pretty good, how about yourself?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I'm doing great! What forms of entertainment do you like?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Action, drama, adventure and comedy as long as it is in fact funny. you?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I prefer drama and comedy for films. I think Jim Carrey is wrong for eschewing drama. I think it's a form that can project positivity.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I agree! especially because he tried to do some serious stuff that wasn't comedy. Sunshine of a spotless mind was strange.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I never saw that, but I heard it was surreal.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Jon Hamm taught drama at his old high school. I believe one of his students was a man named Ellie Kemper who played in the office.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I guess Jon has the magic touch! He trains his students well. Michael Caine joined his high school drama club for the women. I understand that motivation, too.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Well theres a debate about what really makes the world go round, for men at least. 33BC was the earliest works of drama. they called it dramatic theory.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Drama will migrate more and more to video games, I expect. Do you play video games?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I do and let me tell you it already has. its almost like watching a movie now.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I don't play video games, but I can expect that film and games will merge and blend more. People like interactivity, but they want to be entertained as well.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: we come a long way the Nintendo system could only display 54 colors.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: 54 colors is so few, but the game designs were solid. That's what made games on the NES fun. I think the newer systems are more powerful, but not necessary more fun.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Apparently in Caesars day great Britain breed chickens for entertainment.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: The Britons must have had other sources of animal protein. Or they were easily entertained.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: The highest paid actor ever for drama was paid 700,000 an episode.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Hugh Laurie is a legend, and he does a great American accent.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I agree not like some others
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: LOL! Well, I enjoyed chatting with you!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: You as well.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Bye!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Ronald reagan was rejected for a role as president. go figure! have a good day.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Have you used spotify?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I tried it out once, but didn't stick with it. I liked that it's basic features were free. I couldn't find songs I liked though
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah there is  a lot of songs on the service. Did you know that is has over 4 million songs that haven't even been played once? That's apparently 20 percent of their songs!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That is crazy! 4 million unplayed! With so many millions, I should give it a try again. Maybe I should try the paid subscription, with improved streaming quality?
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: It might help, but with so many unplayed songs it may be an indicator there's too many. After all, it takes 130,745 plays per day for a musician to earn minimum wage on the site.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's a shame for the musicians. I 'm surprised Google hasn't come up with competitor yet. Did you realize Google is over 20 years old now. It was founded in 1998!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Actually I did know of that, but did you know that google's map changes depending on the country you live in due to boarder disputes?
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Interesting. Probably saves them from getting hate mail! Hard to believe it started with just two guys, Larry Page and Sergey Brin. They only own 14% of the shares now, though.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Yeah, but I'm sure that's quite substantial considering how big they are. They went down once in 2013 and decrease all web traffic by 40 percent!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: That's nice. It's nice, too, that they at least pretend to care about the earth. They once used live goats to mow the lawns around the headquarters.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: That's definitely a good thing, considering 80 percent of earth's natural forest's have been eradicated.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I wonder how that looks on Google maps! Only 29% of the earth is even land.  The other 71% is water.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Ah yes that is true isn't it? Well I must go now, good talking to you.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: This conversation was so short! Reminds me of how if the earth's history was a 24 hour period, humans would only represent 1 minute and 17 seconds!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Oh wow, that puts things into perspective.
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: Such a short period, and so much has changed on earth in that time. Days used to be only 5.5 hours long when earth was first formed.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Crazy to think we went from that to now facing the complete elimination of helium in our atmosphere
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: I hope when extraterrestrials come they don't need the helium! Maybe we should talk to the United Nations' Ambassador to extraterrestrials. Can you believe that's a real job?!
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: I had no idea, how strange!
edus.speaker: agent_1

edus.text: It sure is! I guess maybe they'll show up some time, even though earth is the only astronomical object known to harbor life.
edus.speaker: agent_2

edus.text: Ah yes, but who knows considering the vastness of space? Had an interesting conversation, good day.
edus.speaker: agent_1
